retrospective.harder_than_needed: The DD-9 re-add-guard removal (deleting the silent unarchiveContact call in addContactByNpub) broke a previously-passing test outside this story's declared scope.includes test-file list: app/tests/unit/contacts-mutation-gaps.test.ts's Gap-3 describe block, which pinned the OLD reactivate-on-readd behavior end to end. Updating it was unavoidable under Regression Prevention's zero-new-failures bar, but it meant touching a file the story spec didn't name. Separately, the AddContactResult return-shape choice for 'blocked' was constrained by an out-of-scope consumer: app/src/lib/processContactInput.ts forwards addResult.error into its own narrower AddContactSubmissionResult error union, so a new error: 'blocked' literal would have broken that file's typecheck. Chose an additive blocked?: boolean + pubkeyHex?: string on the existing already_exists case instead, which typechecks cleanly against the unmodified consumer.
retrospective.surprised_by: AC-SCOPE-3 names four purge/sweep functions across four different files (contacts.ts, unreadStore.ts, chatPersistence.ts, reactions/api.ts), but this story's declared scope only touches one of those files. The other three are argued safe-by-construction (they key exclusively off the untouched isAllowedDmSender, verified by a zero-diff, zero-isBlockedPeer-reference grep on walledGarden.ts) rather than directly regression-tested here — flagged as VQ-S1-023 in verification.json for the examiner, with a suggested landing spot in S3 if dedicated tests turn out to be required.

accepted_residuals: AC-SCOPE-3: only purgeStrangerContacts has a dedicated regression test; the other 3 sweeps (unreadStore/chatPersistence/reactions) have ZERO production diff and are safe-by-construction (key off isAllowedDmSender which returns true for blocked-but-known peers). Accepted sev-3 coverage gap.
contacts-mutation-gaps.test.ts edited outside declared scope.includes: necessary DD-9 ripple (pins the exact removed behavior), test-only, transparently reasoned. Accepted (not a scope violation).
